mnemonics.title: SYSTEM CALL CATEGORIES
mnemonics.content: P-FIDI-COP: Process Control, File management, Device management, Information maintenance, Communication, Protection - the 6 categories of system calls.

mnemonics.title: FORK EXEC SEQUENCE
mnemonics.content: FORK first to CREATE a child, then EXEC to REPLACE its program - remember fork() comes before exec() in process creation.

mnemonics.title: SYSTEM CALL FLOW
mnemonics.content: USER to KERNEL: Store params in registers, trap to OS, execute service, return results, back to user mode.

acronyms.usage: Library functions that provide an abstraction layer above system calls. For example, fopen() is an API that internally calls the open() system call.
A family of standards specifying a portable interface for Unix-like systems, defining a standardized set of system calls.
The fundamental interface through which user programs request operating system services.
A unique number assigned to each process, obtained via getpid() system call.
A condition returned by read() when no more data is available from a file or pipe.

visualTips.visualization: Picture a security guard checkpoint: user mode is outside (restricted), kernel mode is inside (full access). A system call is like presenting your pass to cross from outside to inside.
Think of fork() like cell division - one cell (parent) becomes two identical cells (parent and child), each continuing independently.
Think of exec() like changing the software on your phone - the hardware (process) stays the same, but the program (software) is completely replaced.
Imagine file descriptors as numbered parking slots. When you open a file, you get a slot number (fd). When you close it, that slot is freed for reuse.

analogies.analogy: System call is like ordering food from a restaurant kitchen (requires going to the kitchen area), while a function call is like preparing food in your own kitchen (stays in your space).
Kernel mode is like being the bank manager with full vault access, while user mode is like being a customer who can only use the counter services.
The system call interface is like the reception desk in a hotel - guests (user programs) request services through reception, which coordinates with the staff (kernel).
Passing parameters to system calls is like filling out a form at a government office - you put your request details (parameters) in designated fields (registers) and submit it.
